Security: Sensitive user data is written to an unencrypted file.
No authentication protections such as password hashing or rate limiting.
Performance: User objects are copied unnecessarily in the login loop.
Nested loops in processUsers() create excessive processing overhead.
Linear search for authentication becomes slow as users grow.
Scalability: Authentication uses O(n) search across all users.
Flat file storage does not scale for large datasets.
Entire user dataset is kept in memory.

Bias: Loan approval decision is based on ethnicity.
Privacy: Personally identifiable information is stored without protection.
User credentials are exposed in persistent storage.

Maintainability: No separation of concerns between UI, storage, and business logic.
Lack of error handling for file operations.
